name: Commercial Bank Of Mott
description_short: Community bank operating since 1940 offering traditional banking services, online banking, and lending products with local, personalized service.
description_long:
Commercial Bank of Mott has served its community since 1940, positioning itself as a locally-rooted alternative to large national banks. The institution emphasizes that its employees are community members who know customers by name, creating a personal banking relationship that distinguishes it from impersonal corporate competitors. The bank has modernized its offerings to include both traditional in-person services and contemporary digital channels.

The bank provides a comprehensive array of deposit and loan products alongside an extensive service menu. Core offerings include checking and savings accounts, online banking with bill pay capability, mobile deposit, ATM access, debit cards, credit cards, wire transfers, and safe deposit boxes. They also offer specialized services such as night depository boxes available 24 hours, direct deposit setup, notary services, US Savings Bonds, and money orders. This breadth of services enables customers to handle most routine banking and financial needs through a single institution.

Commercial Bank of Mott's primary distinction is its emphasis on community integration and personalized service. The marketing materials explicitly contrast their approach with larger banks, claiming to offer "most of the services of a big bank, but don't act like one." Their willingness to highlight local employment and accessibility (encouraging customers to "stop in and visit") reflects a traditional community banking model. The availability of multiple contact methods, including a direct phone number (701-824-2593), reinforces accessibility.

As a regional community bank, the institution serves customers who prioritize relationship banking and local presence over potentially competitive rates or specialized fintech features. The website provides essential information about services and security (ID theft information, privacy policy), though it lacks specific detail about account features, fee structures, or interest rates that would allow comprehensive comparison with competitors.
services: Online banking with account access and management
pros: Established community bank with 80+ years of operating history and local reputation since 1940
Comprehensive service menu including online banking, bill pay, mobile deposit, and ATM access
24-hour night depository available for after-hours cash deposits
Free notary service for customers, reducing need for external service providers
Local employees who are community members, enabling personalized relationship banking
Multiple service channels including online, mobile, drive-up, and in-person banking
Direct phone support available for card issues and general inquiries
cons: No transparent fee structure or interest rate information published on website
Limited geographic reach as a regional/community bank may restrict access for customers relocating
Website lacks details about minimum balance requirements, APYs, or loan terms
No information provided about FDIC insurance coverage or regulatory status
Potentially higher fees and lower competitive rates compared to larger regional or national banks
best_for: Long-term community residents who value personal banking relationships and local presence
Small business owners seeking community-based lending and local decision-making
Customers who prefer in-person banking and relationship-based service over digital-only banks
Individuals seeking comprehensive traditional banking services in a single institution
diagnosis: Commercial Bank of Mott is best for community-oriented customers in North Dakota who prioritize personalized, relationship-based banking and prefer supporting a local institution. The main caveat is the lack of published pricing, rate, and fee information on their website, making it difficult to assess competitiveness relative to larger banks without direct contact.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
